       throw std::runtime_error(conn_result.error().what());
     }
     auto conn = conn_result.value();

     auto query = sqlgen::read<std::vector<PurchaseHistory>>
                  | sqlgen::where("customer_id"_c == customer_id)
                  | sqlgen::order_by("order_date"_c.desc());

     auto result = query(conn);
     if (!result) {
       throw std::runtime_error(result.error().what());
     }
     return result.value();
   }

   }  // namespace query_customer_purchase_history

 We must note that the column `customer_id` is used in the condition, but the `PurchaseHistory` struct does not have it. The ORM must be able to handle that.

```cpp
#pragma once

#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>
#include <vector>
#include <optional>

namespace analyze_social_network {

struct User {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> user_id;
  std::string username;
  std::string email;
  std::string join_date; // Assuming string for simplicity, but should be datetime
  std::string city;
};

struct Friendship {
  int user1_id;
  int user2_id;
  sqlgen::Timestamp<"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S"> created_at;
  // We need to define the primary key as composite
  // But the problem says: PRIMARY KEY (user_id1, user_id2)
  // We can use sqlgen::PrimaryKey<std::tuple<int, int>> but that might be complex.
  // Alternatively, we can let sqlgen handle the composite primary key by defining the table appropriately.
};

// We need to define the tables using sqlgen's table macros.
// But the provided code does not have the SQLGEN_TABLE macro. We must add it.

// Let's define the tables:
SQLGEN_TABLE(User, user_id, username, email, join_date, city);
SQLGEN_TABLE(Friendship, user1_id, user2_id, created_at);

struct FriendConnection {
  std::string user1_name;
  std::string user2_name;
};

/**
 * Analyze social network data by creating temporary SQL tables for User and Friendship.
 * Returns: std::vector<FriendConnection>.
 */
std::vector<FriendConnection> analyze_social_network_impl(const std::vector<User>& users, const std::vector<Friendship>& friendships) {
  // Create an in-memory database connection
  auto conn = sqlgen::sqlite::connect(":memory:");

  // Create the tables
  conn->execute("CREATE TABLE User (user_id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, username TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE, email TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE, join_date TEXT NOT NULL, city TEXT);");
  conn->execute("CREATE TABLE Friendship (user1_id INTEGER, user2_id INTEGER, created_at TEXT, PRIMARY KEY (user1_id, user2_id), FOREIGN KEY (user1_id) REFERENCES User(user_id), FOREIGN KEY (user2_id) REFERENCES User(user_id));");

  // Insert the provided data
  for (const auto& user : users) {
    conn->execute("INSERT INTO User (user_id, username, email, join_date, city) VALUES (" + 
                  std::to_string(user.user_id) + ", '" + user.username + "', '" + user.email + "', '" + user.join_date + "', '" + user.city + "');");
  }

  for (const auto& friendship : friendships) {
    conn->execute("INSERT INTO Friendship (user1_id, user2_id, created_at) VALUES (" + 
                  std::to_string(friendship.user1_id) + ", " + std::to_string(friendship.user2_id) + ", '" + friendship.created_at + "');");
  }

  // Build the mutual friends query
  using namespace sqlgen::literals;
  auto query = select_from<User, "u1"_t1>("username"_t1)
    | inner_join<Friendship, "f1"_t2>("u1"_t1("user_id") == "f1"_t2("user1_id"))
    | inner_join<User, "u2"_t3>("f1"_t2("user2_id") == "u2"_t3("user_id"))
    | where("u1"_t1("user_id") < "u2"_t3("user_id"))
    | group_by("u1"_t1("user_id"), "u2"_t3("user_id"))
    | having(count("f1"_t2("user_id2")) > 0)
    | select("u1"_t1("username"), "u2"_t3("username"));

  // Execute the query and map the results
  auto result = query(conn);
  std::vector<FriendConnection> connections;
  for (auto& row : result) {
    FriendConnection fc;
    fc.user1_name = row["u1_username"].get<std::string>();
    fc.user2_name = row["u2_username"].get<std::string>();
    connections.push_back(fc);
  }

  return connections;
}

}  // namespace analyze_social_network
```

        namespace replace_text_in_notes {

        struct UpdatedNote {
          sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> note_id;
          std::string updated_content;
        };

        std::vector<UpdatedNote> replace_text_in_notes() {
          using namespace sqlgen::literals;

          // Connect to the database
          auto conn = sqlgen::sqlite::connect("test_db.sqlite").value();

          // Read all notes
          auto notes = sqlgen::read<std::vector<Note>>(conn).value();

          // Update each note
          for (auto& note : notes) {
            // Replace "foo" with "bar"
            note.content = note.content.replace("foo", "bar");

            // Create an update statement for this note
            auto update_stmt = sqlgen::update<Note>(note.content.set(note.content))
                              | sqlgen::where("note_id"_c == note.note_id);

            // Execute the update
            update_stmt(conn).value();
          }

          // Read the updated notes
          auto updated_notes = sqlgen::read<std::vector<Note>>(conn).value();

          // Convert to UpdatedNote
          std::vector<UpdatedNote> result;
          for (auto& note : updated_notes) {
            result.push_back(UpdatedNote{.note_id = note.note_id, .updated_content = note.content});
          }

          return result;
        }
